title: CODA
logline: A teenage girl from a deaf fishing family in Gloucester discovers her passion for singing while serving as their interpreter, forcing her to choose between her musical dreams and her family's dependence on her.
antagonistOrConflict: The conflict between Ruby's musical aspirations and her family's dependence on her as their only hearing member

actBreaks.description: Ruby commits to pursuing music seriously by asking her mother about joining choir, establishing the central conflict between her dreams and family obligations.

validation.ruling: Ruby defiantly tells her mother she joined choir and is making friends, creating an irreversible shift where she openly challenges family expectations and asserts her independence for the first time.

actBreaks.description: The family faces the Coast Guard hearing crisis and Ruby must choose between her Berklee audition and helping save the family business.

validation.ruling: Ruby sacrifices her musical dreams by deciding to stay and work on the boat after Frank's license suspension, representing the major plot twist that forces her into the highest stakes conflict between family duty and personal aspirations.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic three-act structure but with the intimate pacing of a character study. The story builds through small, authentic moments rather than big plot beats, reflecting the working-class fishing community setting. The structure mirrors Ruby's gradual awakening to her musical gift - starting with casual singing on the boat, progressing through choir discovery, private lessons, and culminating in the Berklee audition. Each act deepens the central tension between Ruby's emerging identity as a singer and her role as the family's essential link to the hearing world.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately measured in Act 1, establishing the rhythms of fishing life and family dynamics. Act 2 accelerates as Ruby's musical development intensifies alongside the family's business struggles, with shorter scenes creating mounting pressure. The final act slows for emotional resolution, allowing space for Ruby's audition performance and the family's ultimate understanding.

incitingIncident.description: Bernardo introduces himself to the new choir class and asks students to sing Happy Birthday to place their voices, but Ruby runs out when called upon.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - DAY

validation.ruling: Ruby joins choir class where she's introduced to music and singing for the first time, shattering her ordinary world of just being the family interpreter and establishing her main conflict between personal dreams and family obligations.

midpoint.description: A retired fisherman hands the Rossis keys to a boarded up warehouse and Jackie signs the paperwork.

validation.ruling: Scene #53 is too minor (just getting warehouse keys). Scene #46 shows Ruby and her family having a tense confrontation about her increasing involvement in music versus family responsibilities, representing the point of no return where she can no longer balance both worlds easily.

climax.description: Ruby realizes she forgot her sheet music and must sing a cappella, but Bernardo arrives to accompany her on piano, saving her audition.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - AUDITORIUM - DAY

validation.ruling: Ruby performs her crucial Berklee audition with Bernardo's surprise assistance, representing the peak dramatic tension where she must prove herself musically and choose her future, directly confronting her ultimate obstacle of pursuing her dreams despite family needs.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - FISHING WATERS - DAWN
scenes.summary: Ruby sings along to Etta James while operating the winch on her family's fishing boat as Frank and Leo work on deck.

scenes.heading: INT. ANGELA ROSE WHEELHOUSE - OCEAN - DAWN, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby enters the wheelhouse and responds to a radio call asking if they're coming in.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE - ON THE WATER - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Angela Rose sails past the breakwall into Gloucester harbor as Ruby watches the shoreline.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALGADO FISH WHARF - DAY
scenes.summary: The family docks and unloads their catch while Arthur threatens to cut off their ice and Tony Salgado weighs their haul.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE - SALGADO FISH WHARF - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby approaches Frank and Leo at the boat and translates Tony's complaints in sign language, revealing her family is deaf.

scenes.heading: EXT. GLOUCESTER STREETS - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby bikes to school through changing neighborhoods while listening to Etta James on headphones.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby arrives late at the high school and runs up the empty front steps.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby falls asleep in history class and accidentally signs while speaking when Ms. Simon wakes her up, embarrassing herself.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - LOCKERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Gertie tells Ruby about hooking up with someone nicknamed Tiny Fingers while Audra makes a snide comment as she passes.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CAFETERIA - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and Gertie sign up for electives in the cafeteria line while Ruby glances at Miles nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby's parents pick her up from school blasting gangsta rap music, mortifying her in front of other students.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S OFFICE - EXAM ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby translates for her parents at a doctor's appointment about their intimate medical condition, becoming increasingly mortified.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROSSI HOME - DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby tries to do homework while her noisy deaf family cooks and eats dinner, then witnesses her parents arguing about declined credit cards and potentially selling the boat.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Bernardo introduces himself to the new choir class and asks students to sing Happy Birthday to place their voices, but Ruby runs out when called upon.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby's first encounter with choir and her musical talent being discovered, setting up the central conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSHIP QUARRY - WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby hikes alone to a granite quarry and sings Happy Birthday, her voice echoing beautifully off the quarry walls.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - KITCHEN/DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby does laundry and secretly watches her parents argue heatedly about declined credit cards and potentially selling the boat.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - OPEN OCEAN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ruby cleans fish on the boat while watching Frank sit alone on the rail, smoking and looking lost in thought.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALGADO FISH WHARF - LATE MORNING
scenes.summary: The family joins a crowd of fishermen listening to Tony Salgado explain new government observer requirements that will monitor their catches.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby approaches Bernardo alone in the choir room and explains she got scared singing in front of others because she used to be made fun of for talking funny.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - DECK/KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jackie asks Ruby to call Grandma while Gertie visits, and Leo tells his mother to leave Ruby alone since she has a friend over.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - RUBY’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gertie asks about Leo's muscles while Ruby plays a Shaggs record and warns Gertie not to date her brother.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - STAIRS/LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gertie signs something to Leo as she leaves, and Ruby discovers Gertie accidentally told Leo she has herpes.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Bernardo calls Ruby to the front of choir class to demonstrate proper breathing technique while singing, and she successfully sings with improved technique.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Bernardo assigns Ruby and Miles to perform a duet of 'You're All I Need to Get By' for the Fall Concert.

scenes.heading: EXT. GLOUCESTER STREETS - SEQUENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby bikes through Gloucester's scenic but economically struggling landscape while listening to the assigned duet song.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALGADO’S PROCESSING - PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby arrives at Salgado Seafood Company and parks her bike.

scenes.heading: INT. SALGADO’S PROCESSING - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby walks through the fish auction, overhears Tony negotiating prices, and approaches Leo and Gio to negotiate their fish sale.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALGADO’S PROCESSING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby finds Frank smoking marijuana and argues with Leo about her interference in the fish negotiations until Frank breaks up their fight.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The choir performs 'It's Your Thing' with Bernardo enthusiastically conducting and handing out percussion instruments.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles attempt their duet but Bernardo stops them, insisting they must practice together and face each other while singing the love song.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - DECK - BREAKFAST
scenes.summary: Ruby tells Jackie she joined choir, and Jackie dismissively compares it to a blind person's child wanting to paint, leading to an argument.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE - SALGADO FISH WHARF - DAY
scenes.summary: Leo pitches starting a fishing co-op to Frank, who explodes that other fishermen see them as a joke because they're deaf.

scenes.heading: INT. PRATTY’S BAR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Leo sits with fishermen at Pratty's Bar but struggles to follow their fast-paced conversation and feels excluded.

scenes.heading: INT. PRATTY’S BAR - LATER
scenes.summary: Leo sits alone at the bar with a black eye while the bartender speaks loudly and slowly to him, and Gertie approaches to check on him.

scenes.heading: INT. PRATTY’S BAR - STORAGE CLOSET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Leo and Gertie make out in the bar's storage closet.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROSSI HOME - DRIVEWAY - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Miles and Ruby walk into the Rossi family's cluttered yard as Ruby apologizes for her gross house and Miles shares his parents' criticism of his guitar playing.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - HALLWAY/RUBY’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Miles and Ruby go to her room where he admires her Shaggs record and shares a childhood memory of seeing her order for her parents at a restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - HALLWAY/PARENTS’ ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby flickers the lights on and off outside her parents' bedroom door.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - PARENTS’ ROOM/HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank and Jackie freeze like busted teenagers when the bedroom lights flicker.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles sit awkwardly with Frank and Jackie in bathrobes as Frank interrogates Miles about his intentions and both parents give explicit safe sex advice.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CAFETERIA - LUNCH
scenes.summary: Ruby carries her lunch tray past Audra and her friends who make sex noises and mime putting on a condom, realizing Miles told someone about the encounter.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby confronts Miles in the hallway about telling people, and he admits he told Jay because he thought it was funny.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby runs into the stairwell alone and cries.

scenes.heading: INT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Bernardo works with Ruby at his piano on 'Both Sides Now,' pushing her to connect emotionally with the song and let her voice out.

scenes.heading: INT. CITY HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: At a Fisheries Council meeting, fishermen including Frank and Leo argue with officials about quotas and government monitors while Ruby sneaks in to join them.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Rossi family discusses Frank's impulsive announcement about starting a co-op, with Jackie expressing skepticism about the work and money involved.
scenes.turningPointNote: The family commits to starting their own fishing business, which will increase Ruby's responsibilities and deepen the central conflict

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - RUBY’S ROOM - EARLY MORNING, STILL DARK
scenes.summary: Ruby's alarm goes off at 3 am and she gets ready for fishing, playing The Clash loudly while preparing her clothes.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - PARENTS’ ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby enters her parents' room where their bed vibrates and lights flash as an alarm, and she shakes Frank awake.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - OPEN OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: On the boat, Leo hoses the deck while Ruby studies classical sheet music and practices Italian pronunciation until Leo douses her with the hose.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALGADO FISH WHARF - DAY
scenes.summary: The Rossi family sets up a folding table at the wharf and passes out flyers for their new Fresh Catch business.

scenes.heading: EXT. GLOUCESTER - VARIOUS LOCATIONS - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby goes from boat to boat talking to fishermen about joining the Fresh Catch Program, with several refusing but a few signing up.

scenes.heading: INT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Bernardo teaches Ruby music notation terms like forte, fortissimo, piano, and pianissimo.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: A retired fisherman hands the Rossis keys to a boarded up warehouse and Jackie signs the paperwork.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Rossis secure their warehouse for the Fresh Catch business, establishing their new venture

scenes.heading: INT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Ruby sings 'The Marriage of Figaro' while Bernardo teaches her breathing technique, then he notices she smells fishy.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Rossis clean the dingy warehouse while Ruby hums and looks at sheet music, with Jackie observing the growing distance between them.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERNARDO'S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby ignores Miles as she arrives late to her lesson, while Bernardo appears annoyed and Miles tries to apologize.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - BACK DECK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Ruby and Bernardo practice clapping rhythms on his back deck while his daughter Misha plays nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank spray paints 'FRESH CATCH' on the warehouse wall while Ruby enters and sees Leo and Gertie making out, looking disgusted.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and Jackie work with fishermen's wives to fill orders and package fish, with Ruby juggling phone calls while Jackie feels left out of the women's jokes.

scenes.heading: INT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby falls asleep on the floor during a breathing exercise lesson with Bernardo.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bernardo finds Ruby on the phone conducting business and tells her to get inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Bernardo confronts Ruby about being late three times, threatening to stop working with her if she doesn't respect his time.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - LOCKERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Miles tries to apologize to Ruby at her locker, claiming he wasn't laughing at her parents for being deaf and mentioning problems at home.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - LOADING DOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby manages fish packaging with the fishermen's wives who joke about exotic food orders while Jackie watches with a polite smile, then Ruby leaves for music practice.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ruby tries to leave for music practice but Jackie stops her when a news crew arrives to film a story about the family.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby interprets for Frank and Jackie during a news interview while texting Bernardo about being late, causing Leo to kick her chair in annoyance.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Ruby races to Bernardo's house but finds it locked with no answer, leaving her sitting defeated on the stoop.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - CHOIR ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby finds Bernardo playing piano alone and apologizes, but he refuses to continue teaching her, calling her undisciplined and telling her to leave.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bernardo fires Ruby as a student, potentially ending her Berklee dreams

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby tells her family she wants to attend Berklee music school, stunning them, while they argue about losing their interpreter and the new business.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby reveals her college aspirations to her family, creating major conflict

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - PARENTS’ ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and Jackie argue in bed about Ruby's college plans, with Jackie worried about Ruby failing and leaving them.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - RUBY’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby sets her alarm for 3am and screams in frustration, then receives a text from Miles offering to make amends.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - SALGADO FISH WHARF - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Frank and Leo prep the boat while a middle-aged woman named Joanne Biles arrives as their new at-sea monitor.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. ANGELA ROSE DECK/WHEELHOUSE - OPEN OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: The Angela Rose heads out to sea with Joanne aboard while Frank eats peanut butter and Leo jokes about throwing her overboard.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSHIP QUARRY - WOODS - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles hike to a quarry where Ruby strips down and leaps off a thirty-foot cliff into the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - OPEN OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Joanne tries to communicate with Frank about engine maintenance, but he writes 'Deaf' on her notebook to explain why he can't hear her.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSHIP QUARRY - TRAIL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles jump off the cliff together into the quarry water after Ruby warns him about the dangers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - OPEN OCEAN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Frank and Leo work on deck while Coast Guard radio transmissions go unanswered because no one sees the warning light in the empty wheelhouse except Joanne, who doesn't alert them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Coast Guard tries to contact the boat but can't reach the deaf crew, setting up a major crisis

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSHIP QUARRY - WATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles float on a log in the quarry, play together, then share a perfect kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby returns home to find her family arguing because Frank's fishing license was suspended after they failed to respond to the Coast Guard.
scenes.turningPointNote: The family's livelihood is destroyed due to Ruby's absence from the boat

scenes.heading: INT. COAST GUARD OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Coast Guard officers fine Frank $2,500 for failing to obey orders and operating dangerously due to his disability, requiring a hearing crew member to fish again.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ruby offers to give up college and work on the boat permanently, while Leo angrily calls her 'Saint Ruby' and storms off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby sacrifices her dreams to save the family business

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - RUBY’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jackie brings Ruby a red dress for the concert and they have an intimate conversation where Ruby asks if Jackie ever wishes she was deaf.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: Leo tells Ruby she can't stay and fish full-time because her singing talent is special, erupting in frustration about wanting to handle things as the older brother.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - AUDITORIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby peeks through the curtain at her parents in the audience while Bernardo prepares students for the concert performance.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - AUDITORIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Rossi family sits in the auditorium as Bernardo introduces the concert, with Jackie asking Leo to read lips since she can't understand.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - AUDITORIUM - LATER
scenes.summary: Ruby and Miles perform on stage while the scene shifts to Frank and Jackie's perspective, showing only silence as they watch Ruby's lips move without hearing her voice.
scenes.turningPointNote: The audience experiences Ruby's performance from her deaf parents' perspective, revealing their isolation

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL - AUDITORIUM - LOBBY - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: After the concert, Bernardo meets Ruby's family and accidentally signs an obscene phrase instead of 'nice to meet you,' having learned it from YouTube.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROSSI HOME - DRIVEWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank and Ruby sit on the truck tailgate looking at stars while Ruby sings the concert song for him privately, explaining it's about sacrifice and needing someone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Frank experiences Ruby's voice for the first time through her private performance

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - RUBY’S ROOM - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Frank wakes Ruby up early in the morning, telling her they're not going fishing.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROSSI HOME - DRIVEWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: The entire Rossi family excitedly piles into the car together for a trip.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI CAR - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby watches the New England landscape pass from the car window and texts Bernardo that she's coming.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The family car speeds along the highway toward Boston's skyline.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Rossis arrive at Berklee and jump out of the car, with Frank going to park while the others run for the entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - CHECK IN - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby checks in for her audition feeling out of place in her shabby sweater, learning she's late and that her family can't accompany her inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - WAITING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby waits nervously outside the auditorium listening to an intimidating opera singer, then sees Miles who tells her Bernardo just left and that he choked in his audition.

scenes.heading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - AUDITORIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The faculty jury questions Ruby about her limited musical background based on her application.

scenes.heading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - LOBBY - SAME
scenes.summary: Frank, Jackie, and Leo sneak up to the balcony to watch Ruby's audition.

scenes.heading: INT. BERKLEE MUSIC HALL - AUDITORIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby realizes she forgot her sheet music and must sing a cappella, but Bernardo arrives to accompany her on piano, saving her audition.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby's audition is saved when Bernardo unexpectedly arrives to accompany her

scenes.heading: EXT. ANGELA ROSE DECK - OPEN OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby works on the boat with a new deckhand while Frank angrily shows him how to sort fish, making Ruby laugh as she realizes she'll miss this life.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Ruby teaches Miles how to sign his name as they sit together on a stone wall at sunset.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - LOADING DOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank gives Leo shit while they load boxes of fish into a new truck painted with the Fresh Catch logo.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARM STAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Jackie works with fishermen's wives Nina and Angela distributing fish at a farmers' market, counting cash and laughing with them as she's now included in their jokes.

scenes.heading: INT. PRATTY’S BAR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Leo and Frank enter Pratty's Bar where other fishermen greet them with waves and they join the group.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby and her family crowd around the computer as she logs into the Berklee website to check her acceptance status, waiting anxiously while staring at the screen.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BERNARDO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby bikes to Bernardo's house and runs up the steps, where Bernardo opens the door and looks at her nervously, awaiting the verdict.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSSI HOME - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby clicks the final button on her computer revealing her Berklee acceptance, causing her to whoop with joy as Frank, Jackie and Leo erupt in cheers and celebration.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby's acceptance to Berklee resolves the central question of whether she can pursue her musical dreams

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSHIP QUARRY - THE BARGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Miles and Ruby sit on The Barge discussing her move to Boston, then hold hands and leap off the cliff together into the water, emerging and howling to the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROSSI HOME - DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Ruby efficiently hugs her family goodbye by the packed car, declining their offer to help set up her dorm room and trying to avoid making it an emotional scene.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. GERTIE’S CAR/ROSSI HOME - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: As Gertie drives Ruby away, Ruby panics and runs back to embrace her family one final time before Frank tells her to go and she returns to the car, waving as they disappear.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruby's final goodbye represents her definitive choice to leave for college while maintaining her family bond
